description: Roles determined by rank, class, or position within a social hierarchy.
roles: Noble
Influence the protagonists need but cannot command, forcing them to play politics or risk making a powerful enemy.
Peasant
The human cost of conflict made visible, grounding epic stakes in the suffering of ordinary lives.
Clergy
Divine authority given a voice, offering blessings or judgment that the faithful around the protagonists take with deadly seriousness.
Outcast
Truths polite society refuses to speak, available to the protagonists -- but only at the cost of association.
Refugee
Living consequences of the conflict the protagonists are trying to resolve, putting a face on what failure truly means.
Criminal
Doors to the underworld opened where lawful means cannot reach, yet every favor accepted deepens the compromise.
Veteran
Scars from battles already fought, offering hard-earned tactical wisdom and a sobering preview of what war costs.
Foreigner
Unfamiliar knowledge and customs reframing problems the protagonists thought they understood, shifting perspective through an outside lens.
Servant
Unseen movement through powerful households, holding overheard secrets that can shift the balance if the protagonists earn their trust.
Elder
Respect the protagonists have not yet earned, gatekeeping community support behind traditions and long memories.
